nodes.label: Yamaguchi 1Day Bus Landing Page Screenshot
nodes.description: UI screenshot of the Yamaguchi 1Day Bus service landing page (Korean-language tourism site). Layout top to bottom: (1) top utility bar with language switcher (English/한국어/簡体字/繁体字), ContactUs, login link; (2) main nav bar with bus-shaped logo icon and 'Yamaguchi 1Day Bus' wordmark, plus nav links 야마구치1Day버스/운행코스/1Day버스+호텔/문의게시판; (3) large hero banner photo of a scenic bridge over blue ocean, overlaid with a departure-days badge ('매주 화 목 금 토 출발!'), bold title '야마구치 1DAY 버스', descriptive subtext, and a horizontal route/stop icon strip (쓰노시마 > 모토노스미 신사 > 유모토온천 > 아키요시다이 전망대 > 루리코지 5층 석탑 > 유다온천 > 가라토 시장) each with a small themed icon (bridge, torii gate, hot spring, mountains, pagoda, fox, shopping bag); a green checkmark callout box on the right lists qualifying conditions for booking (rental car hesitation, high transit costs, difficulty with Google Maps, wanting to see highlights only, language barrier); (4) 'Bus Course' section with a bus-icon heading, weekday/weekend fare card (화·목 4,900엔 / 금·토 5,900엔) with route text and a green '예약하기' (Reserve) button, alongside an 8-photo grid gallery of destination scenery (bridge, red torii pathway, hot spring bath, flower field, cherry blossoms, ferry, blue building, market stall); (5) promotional banner block 'Yamaguchi 1Day Bus 야마구치 관광정보 둘러보기' over a magazine/travel-book photo, paired with a 'Notice' list panel showing five placeholder dated entries (2019-12-31, 텍스트입니다, 운영자); (6) footer with 부관훼리/(주)넷투재팬 company branding, phone contact 051-463-7182, and a Busan port address. Overall design uses a teal/green brand color, card-based sections, rounded pill buttons for departure days, and a tourism-catalog visual style typical of a Korean travel-agency booking site targeting inbound tourists to Yamaguchi, Japan via Busan ferry.
